from source import lcs_length

def test_lcs_length_reveals_dp_update_bug():
    s = "ABCBDAB"
    t = "BDCAB"
    result = lcs_length(s, t)
    # True LCS length is 4 ("BCAB", "BDAB")
    assert result == 4, (
        f"lcs_length('{s}', '{t}') should be 4 (true LCS length), "
        f"but got {result}. The bug (using dp[i-1, j] instead of dp[i-1, j-1]) "
        f"inflates the length by allowing same-column reuse."
    )

attempts.test_code:
from source import lcs_length

def test_lcs_length_correct_substring():
    """
    Verify that lcs_length correctly computes the longest common substring length.
    The buggy code uses dp[i-1, j] instead of dp[i-1, j-1], which either undercounts
    (when characters are not repeated in the same column) or overcounts (when there
    is a run of the same character in s matching a single character in t).
    This test uses strings where no character repeats consecutively in s, so buggy
    returns 1 while the correct answer is 3.
    """
    s = "abc"
    t = "abc"
    result = lcs_length(s, t)
    # Expected: longest common substring is "abc" of length 3
    assert result == 3, f"Expected 3 for lcs_length('{s}', '{t}'), got {result}"
